ocr.text: The role of antidepressant medication in the management of depressive disorders has been a focal point of clinical research,
particularly in examining the efficacy of various interventions. The data presented in Table 1 highlights the comparative
outcomes between two distinct groups: the Intervention group (EERIP) and the Control group (FGL). Both groups were
administered antidepressant medications, allowing for an analysis of their respective effectiveness. The metrics outlined in the
table, specifically the "Lower" and "Upper" confidence intervals, provide critical insights into the variability and reliability of
the results. These intervals are essential for understanding the potential range of effects that the interventions may yield.
Components  Intervention group (EERIP)1  Control group (FGL)2
Antidepressive medication  Yes  Yes
Cognitive or other individual therapy  Yes  Yes
Work orientation  Yes  Yes/No
Family orientation  Yes  Yes/No
Group therapy methods  Yes  No
Duration of intensive interventions  6 months  No recommendation
B  S.E.  p  OR  OR %95 CI
Lower  Upper
Grade  0.001
Furthermore, the significance of the results is underscored by the reported p-value of 0.001, indicating a statistically significant
difference between the two groups. This level of significance suggests that the intervention has a meaningful impact on the
outcomes measured, warranting further investigation into the specific components of the treatment protocols utilized in the
EERIP group. Overall, the findings contribute to the growing body of evidence supporting the tailored use of antidepressant
therapies in clinical settings.
